item.title: John Bolton pleads guilty to retaining classified information
item.description: Bolton agreed to pay a $2.25 million fine, pleading guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information.

item.title: Slow progress toward peace deal as Iran strikes ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Hezbollah says Israel must withdraw forces from Lebanon and Iran strikes a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as negotiations toward a peace deal grind on.

item.title: Cassidy says Trump "was berating" senators during meeting: "I'm not there to be berated"
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana told "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan that President Trump "was berating" the senators who supported the Iran war powers resolution during a meeting. "Frankly, I'm not there to be berated," Cassidy said. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Big Tech is spending trillions on AI. Investors want proof it'll pay off.
item.description: Technology companies are betting trillions of dollars that consumers will open their wallets for AI services. But what if Big Tech is wrong?

item.title: Serena Williams to face Maya Joint in Wimbledon first-round match
item.description: Tennis champion Serena Williams will go up against 20-year-old Maya Joint of Australia in her first match of the Wimbledon tournament. The Athletic's Matt Futterman joins "CBS Morning News" to discuss.

item.title: Apple, Microsoft blame chip shortage for price hikes on electronic products
item.description: Apple and Microsoft announced they're hiking prices for some electronic products, including computers and XBOX consoles, citing a shortage of memory chips. CNET editor-at-large Scott Stein weighs in.

item.title: AI helps decode new secrets from scrolls burned by Mount Vesuvius
item.description: An 18th-century archaeological dig uncovered a library of intact but charred scrolls. Their contents have been unreadable until recently.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense team discussed possible plea deal in federal case, sources say
item.description: Luigi Mangione's attorneys discussed a potential plea agreement before negotiations fell apart, sources said. Mangione pleaded not guilty to state and federal charges after he was arrested for allegedly killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in December 2024. CBS News' Lilia Luciano has the details.

item.title: U.S. team suffers first 2026 World Cup loss, heads to knockout round
item.description: The U.S. men's team had already clinched its spot in the Round of 32, the knockout round, with its 2-0 win over Australia on Friday.

item.title: Inside southern Lebanon as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues
item.description: CBS News traveled to southern Lebanon, visiting the ancient city of Tyre, amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah. Holly Williams spoke with city residents about the conflict.

item.title: Paris bans alcohol in public amid record-breaking heatwave
item.description: Authorities are attempting to reduce the pressure on hospitals as the city swelters under extreme heat.

item.title: Billionaire Leon Black testifying before House Epstein panel
item.description: An investor who employed and was close to Jeffrey Epstein is appearing before members of Congress investigating the deceased sexual abuser.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises amid desperate search for survivors
item.description: Dual earthquakes devastated parts of Venezuela on Wednesday. Now, emergency workers and search and rescue groups are combing through debris and fallen buildings as they look for trapped survivors. CBS News' Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll doubles
item.description: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed almost 600 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

item.title: Venezuela quake survivors describe devastation: "Everything collapsed"
item.description: A Caracas resident told CBS News that he "started to pray" when he felt the first earthquake hit Venezuela.

item.title: Royals reveal personal tax bills
item.description: The British royal family started paying income tax in 1992, but this year they revealed how much they actually paid. Meanwhile, King Charles is also upending royal tradition, saying he and Queen Camilla won't live at Buckingham Palace after its nearly 10-year renovation is completed.

item.title: U.S. men's team looks ahead to World Cup knockout round after loss to Turkey
item.description: Turkey stunned the U.S. Men's National Team in their final group match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The team will still proceed to the Round of 32 after winning the first two matches of the tournament. Former USMNT player Marcelo Balboa joins "CBS Morning News" to discuss.

item.title: Europeans seek relief from extreme temperatures amid deadly heat wave
item.description: Brutal temperatures are straining hospitals and other services amid a deadly heat wave continuing to sweep across western Europe. In France, drowning deaths have increased to 55 as people seek relief from the heat. Chris Livesay reports.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers discussed possible plea deal in federal trial, but talks fell apart
item.description: Luigi Mangione's attorneys and federal prosecutors were discussing a possible plea deal in his federal case, but talks fell apart. Mangione is accused of fatally shooting UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in 2024 and has pleaded not guilty.

item.title: Inside Lebanon as fighting continues between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: Violence has continued in Lebanon despite the U.S.-Iran agreement. Holly Williams went to Lebanon to see the destruction firsthand and spoke to those caught in the crossfire.

item.title: Iran attacks container ship in Strait of Hormuz, potentially impacting peace deal
item.description: Iran has attacked a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz, impacting oil prices and potentially threatening efforts to fully reopen the strait. Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Venezuela's medical centers "absolutely overwhelmed" after twin earthquakes, aid group chief says
item.description: The death toll is still climbing after two 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday, destroying buildings and other infrastructure. CBS News' Jarred Hill reports, and Project HOPE president Chris Skopec discusses international aid efforts.

item.title: A look at the catastrophic damage after 2 powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela
item.description: Rescue crews are digging through the rubble for survivors after Wednesday's back-to-back earthquakes that struck Venezuela. Cristian Benavides gives a look at the damage and rescue efforts.

item.title: Massive wildfires grow across Utah as conditions worsen
item.description: Large wildfires in Utah continue to grow as dangerous winds and severe drought make them more difficult to fight. Rob Marciano reports.

item.title: Exonerated man's widow calls settlement in yogurt shop murders "blood money"
item.description: The city of Austin agreed to a multimillion-dollar settlement to be split among four men — including to the widow and daughter of Maurice Pierce — who were wrongfully accused of murdering four teenage girls in a Texas yogurt shop.

item.title: King Charles will not live in Buckingham Palace after $487 million renovation
item.description: The king and Queen Camilla have decided instead to continue living at nearby Clarence House, a smaller, stately home.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela, hundreds dead or injured
item.description: Venezuela's acting president said the death toll from powerful twin earthquakes was likely to rise, as USGS modeling suggested thousands may have been killed.

item.title: Mangione's attorneys discussed possible plea deal but talks fell apart, sources say
item.description: Mangione​ is facing both state and federal charges for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's murder in December 2024. He has pleaded not guilty in both cases.

item.title: Judge orders DOJ to either unredact more Epstein files or explain why it can't
item.description: A judge on Thursday ordered the Justice Department to either release unredacted versions of several files on the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein or explain why it can't do so.

item.title: Trump's obsession with SAVE America Act drives Congress into standoff
item.description: The president and his conservative allies have stymied other legislation as they unsuccessfully try to pass a voting regulations bill that lacks even simple majority support in the Senate.
